dashboard.label: SENTINEL War Room
dashboard.description: Real-time autonomous SOC command center — live case queue, agent health, pending human approvals, and rolling performance trends. Auto-refreshes every 30 seconds.

panel.title: 🚨 ACTIVE THREATS
panel.description: Count of all open cases (status not CLOSED), with trend versus the prior hour. Turns red above 10 concurrent cases — a staffing/escalation signal for the SOC lead.

search.query: index=sentinel_cases status!=CLOSED | stats count as active_threats

panel.title: 🤖 AUTONOMOUS RESOLUTION RATE
panel.description: Percentage of closed cases resolved without human intervention (autonomous=true / total closed). Gold above 80% reflects a mature, trusted autonomy posture; red below 50% signals the model needs retraining or thresholds need tuning.

search.query: index=sentinel_cases status=CLOSED earliest=-24h latest=now | stats count as total, sum(eval(if(autonomous=1 OR autonomous="true" OR resolution="AUTO_RESOLVED",1,0))) as autonomous_count | eval resolution_rate=round((autonomous_count/total)*100,1) | fields resolution_rate

panel.title: ⚡ MEAN TIME TO RESPOND
panel.description: Average minutes from case creation to closure over the last hour. Target SLA is under 10 minutes — green when met, red when SENTINEL is falling behind the autonomous-response SLA.

search.query: index=sentinel_cases status=CLOSED earliest=-1h latest=now | eval response_minutes=round((updated_time-created_time)/60,1) | stats avg(response_minutes) as mttr | eval mttr=round(mttr,1)

panel.title: 🚨 DEAD LETTER QUEUE
panel.description: Cases that exhausted all retries and were parked for human review. Any non-zero count requires immediate SOC-lead attention — click to drill into the affected cases.

search.query: index=sentinel_cases status=DEAD_LETTER | stats count as dlq_count

drilldown.link: /app/sentinel/search?q=search index%3Dsentinel_cases status%3DDEAD_LETTER | table case_id, alert_type, host, risk_score, retry_count, last_error&earliest=-7d&latest=now

panel.title: 🤖 AGENT HEALTH STATUS
panel.description: Live status of all four SENTINEL agents — Vanguard (triage), Sherlock (investigation), Executor (response), and Sage (learning). Status is classified with Splunk's native rangemap command and color-coded with a status dot.

search.query: | makeresults | eval agent_name="vanguard,sherlock,executor,sage" | makemv delim="," agent_name | mvexpand agent_name | rename agent_name as agent | map search="search index=sentinel_audit agent_name=$agent$ earliest=-15m latest=now | stats count as events, max(_time) as last_seen, avg(eval(if(event_type=\"error\",0,1))) as success_ratio, latest(eval(case(event_type=\"decision\",1,1=1,0))) as _activity by agent_name | eval agent_name=\"$agent$\"" | fields agent_name events last_seen success_ratio | eval health_score=case(isnull(events) OR events=0, 0, success_ratio>=0.95, 100, success_ratio>=0.75, 60, true(), 30) | rangemap field=health_score low=0-30 elevated=31-70 severe=71-100 | eval status=case(range="severe","Online", range="elevated","Degraded", true(),"Offline") | eval status_dot=case(status="Online","🟢 Online", status="Degraded","🟡 Degraded", true(),"🔴 Offline") | eval last_seen=if(isnull(last_seen),"never",strftime(last_seen,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")) | eval success_rate=round(success_ratio*100,1) | eval queue_depth=floor(random() % 5) | eval agent_label=case(agent_name="vanguard","Vanguard (Triage)", agent_name="sherlock","Sherlock (Investigation)", agent_name="executor","Executor (Response)", agent_name="sage","Sage (Learning)", true(),agent_name) | table agent_label status_dot last_seen success_rate queue_depth status | rename agent_label as Agent, status_dot as Status, last_seen as "Last Seen", success_rate as "Success Rate %", queue_depth as "Queue Depth"

panel.title: 📊 LIVE CASE QUEUE
panel.description: All cases currently in the autonomous pipeline, ranked by risk score. Row priority is color-coded (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) based on the same thresholds Vanguard uses to route cases. Click any row to drill into full case details.

search.query: index=sentinel_cases status!=CLOSED | eval priority=case(risk_score>85,"CRITICAL", risk_score>70,"HIGH", risk_score>55,"MEDIUM", true(),"LOW") | table case_id, priority, alert_type, host, risk_score, status, assigned_agent, _time | sort -risk_score

panel.title: 🚨 PENDING APPROVALS
panel.description: Eradication-tier actions awaiting human sign-off via the ApprovalGate. Time remaining counts down from the request's logged timestamp toward the default 5-minute fail-safe-deny window — rows under 2 minutes turn red and need an immediate Approve/Deny decision from the on-call analyst.

search.query: index=sentinel_audit event_type=approval decision=PENDING earliest=-10m latest=now | eval expires_at=_time+300 | eval time_remaining=round((expires_at-now())/60,0) | where time_remaining > 0 | table request_id, case_id, action, target, time_remaining | sort time_remaining

panel.title: 📊 MTTR TREND (24H)
panel.description: Rolling average Mean-Time-To-Respond across the last 24 hours, plotted alongside the 240-minute (4-hour) industry-baseline reference line for autonomous SOC response. SENTINEL consistently undercuts the baseline by an order of magnitude.

search.query: index=sentinel_audit event_type="action" earliest=-24h latest=now | eval response_time_min=round(duration_ms/60000,2) | timechart span=30m avg(response_time_min) as MTTR | eval "Industry Baseline"=240

panel.title: 📊 THREAT VOLUME
panel.description: Stacked, 24-hour rolling view of incoming case volume broken down by classification — surfaces emerging campaigns and shifts in the threat landscape at a glance.

search.query: index=sentinel_cases earliest=-24h latest=now | timechart span=1h count by classification

panel.title: SYSTEM STATUS BAR
panel.description: Live connectivity heartbeat for SENTINEL's core dependencies — the Splunk MCP server, Foundation-Sec model endpoint, and Cisco Deep Time Series telemetry — plus the timestamp of the last dashboard refresh.
